test: matched-pair forward kernel diff (Test A) — ruling out bf16 kernel as source
method: load ref iter N ckpt into nano, forward on iter N+1 batch, compare to ref's logged single-iter loss

runs.config: bf16 autocast + PyTorch SDPA flash attention

conclusion: Three forward configurations — SDPA bf16, manual fp32 attention, fp32 everywhere — all produce essentially the same +0.0137~0.0139 nat delta vs ref. bf16 kernel accumulation and attention implementation are RULED OUT as the source of mid-training residual.
ruled_out: MoE expert bias not loaded (verified 8 layers x 144 loaded)
remaining_suspects_historical: MoE routing/dispatch SEMANTIC difference (not bf16 rounding) — nano dense-mask vs ref allgather
Weight converter edge case — subtle layout/scale off for some tensor
Loss mask computation — ref's loss_mask logic for EOD/packed-doc boundaries vs nano's eod_mask_loss+mask_loss_id
resolution_v10_2026_04_23: Root cause was neither MoE routing, weight converter, nor loss mask — it was the RoPE implementation. Commit a97ce75 replaced te.apply_rotary_pos_emb with megatron.core.extensions.transformer_engine.fused_apply_rotary_pos_emb and accounted for 95%+ of per-layer systematic drift. Other contributors: 373cbb3 (SwiGLU fp32 silu*up), e8b6c2c (MoE fp32 weighted sum), d0223fe (te.GroupedLinear for expert MLPs), 076fb6d (input-based EOD mask). Post-fix: 7485-iter retrain final Δ=+0.0047 nat, block 0 dense layer is bitwise (L1=2.43e-7). See ALIGNMENT.md v10 FINAL.
